airports.name: Dubai International
airports.story: If airports had personalities, DXB would be that impossibly well-dressed friend who somehow makes a 3am layover feel like a luxury event. I've spent more nights here than I have in some cities I call visited. Terminal 3 at 2am, the whole world is passing through — and somehow it still feels like mine.

airports.name: Haneda Airport
airports.story: Haneda is what airports dream of being. Clean enough to eat off the floor. Staff who bow when you board. A ramen shop at gate 52 that made me miss my connection on purpose, or at least that's my story. Tokyo starts the moment you land — the city doesn't wait for you to clear immigration.

airports.name: Changi Airport
airports.story: Changi has a waterfall. A seven-story indoor waterfall. I've seen it four times and it still stops me cold. The kind of airport that makes you wish your layover was longer. Singapore understood that the airport is the first impression — and they made theirs a masterpiece.

airports.name: London Heathrow
airports.story: Heathrow is the airport equivalent of a commuter train — efficient, slightly tired, everyone has somewhere else to be. But Terminal 5 in the early morning, light coming through those glass panels, coffee in hand — there's a quiet dignity to it. The whole world passes through here too, just with more queuing.

airports.name: Indira Gandhi International
airports.story: Terminal 3 is the airport I grew up through. Every time I land here I remember being seventeen, watching planes take off from a rooftop in my town, wondering where they were going. Now I'm on them. DEL is home base, the reset point, the place that reminds me how far I've come.

airports.name: John F. Kennedy International
airports.story: JFK is barely functional and somehow still iconic. The chaos, the accents, the taxi line that tests your patience — and then you're in Manhattan 45 minutes later and you forget all of it. The TWA Hotel lobby alone is worth a missed connection. Some airports you endure. Some you remember.

flights.notes: Best in-flight food I've ever had
Slept through the whole thing. No regrets.
Landed at dawn. Paris is best at dawn.
